title: Mexican Breakfast Quesadilla

subs.name: large whole-wheat roti or maida paratha for an Indian kitchen swap
paneer, crumbled and pan-fried, for a desi version
subs.notes: paneer does not melt like cheese, so scatter it evenly

subs.name: corn kernels or peas for a sweeter filling

steps.text: Heat 1 tsp oil in a frying pan on medium flame. Add the onion, capsicum and green chilli. Saute for 3–4 minutes until the onion is soft and the capsicum is slightly tender but still has a little bite.

Whisk the eggs with salt, pepper and coriander leaves. Pour over the softened vegetables. Scramble gently, pushing the mixture from the edges to the centre, and remove from heat when the eggs are just barely set — they will finish cooking inside the quesadilla. Add the diced tomato and fold through.

Heat a clean tawa or griddle on medium flame — no oil needed. Place one tortilla on it. Scatter half the grated cheese over one half of the tortilla, then spoon half the egg filling over the cheese. Fold the empty half over to make a half-moon shape.

Press the folded quesadilla lightly with a spatula and cook for 90 seconds until the underside is golden and slightly charred in spots. Flip carefully and cook the other side for another 60–90 seconds until golden and the cheese is melted and bubbling at the seam.

Remove from the tawa, rest for 30 seconds, then cut into triangles with a knife or pizza cutter. Repeat for the second quesadilla. Serve hot with tomato ketchup, green chilli sauce or plain curd on the side.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: tortilla → large whole-wheat roti or maida paratha for an Indian kitchen swap (works beautifully on a tawa); cheese → paneer, crumbled and pan-fried, for a desi version (paneer does not melt like cheese, so scatter it evenly); capsicum → corn kernels or peas for a sweeter filling
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Flour tortillas are available at most Indian metro supermarkets. Alternatively, use store-bought whole-wheat rotis or fresh maida chapatis — they crisp up identically on a tawa. Amul processed cheese or any grated mozzarella works as the melting cheese. A medium-hot gas tawa is ideal; no oil is needed on the tawa for the toasting step as the oil inside the filling prevents sticking. Serve with green chutney or tomato ketchup as dipping sauces.
